description_long: This object is a large, single-edged knife with a pronounced forward curve, resembling a kukri or machete. The blade is dark, possibly coated or oxidized, and features a wide belly that tapers to a sharp point. The handle is ergonomically shaped, with a metallic or composite construction, and includes a prominent circular hole at the pommel, likely for a lanyard or hanging. There is no visible guard between the blade and handle, giving it a streamlined appearance. The overall design suggests a utilitarian tool, suitable for chopping or slashing tasks. The finish is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements.
description: A large, curved, single-edged knife with a dark blade and ergonomic handle featuring a circular pommel hole. The design is functional and robust, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curved blade and ergonomic handle with a circular pommel hole.
description_view_1: Edge-on view displaying the thin profile of the blade and handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the full blade and handle, with the pommel hole visible.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, again showing the thinness of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: ergonomic, metallic or polymer, circular pommel hole, no visible grip texture
